criteria_verification.criterion_text: `GateDecision` references collected from SQLite repo (read-only via
criteria_verification.evidence: PipelineHistoryRepository is declared as a typing.Protocol with a single read method get_gate_decisions_for_build(build_id) -> Sequence[GateDecision]. The module imports nothing from FEAT-FORGE-001 and has no write surface against SQLite. TestGateDecisionCollection::test_repo_queried_with_build_id confirms the repository is queried with the build_id; test_repo_without_required_method_raises_typeerror enforces the Protocol shape at runtime.
criteria_verification.notes: PipelineHistoryRepository is declared as a typing.Protocol with a single read method get_gate_decisions_for_build(build_id) -> Sequence[GateDecision]. The module imports nothing from FEAT-FORGE-001 and has no write surface against SQLite. TestGateDecisionCollection::test_repo_queried_with_build_id confirms the repository is queried with the build_id; test_repo_without_required_method_raises_typeerror enforces the Protocol shape at runtime.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Sort by `decided_at` ASC before constructing `gate_decision_ids` list
criteria_verification.evidence: _sort_gate_decisions returns sorted(decisions, key=lambda d: (d.decided_at, str(d.entity_id))) — primary by decided_at ASC, secondary by entity_id for deterministic tie-breaking when timestamps collide at microsecond resolution. Verified by TestOrderingByDecidedAt::test_out_of_order_decisions_are_sorted_on_output and test_microsecond_tied_timestamps_use_entity_id_tiebreaker; test_pure_helper_does_not_mutate_input pins the immutability contract.
criteria_verification.notes: _sort_gate_decisions returns sorted(decisions, key=lambda d: (d.decided_at, str(d.entity_id))) — primary by decided_at ASC, secondary by entity_id for deterministic tie-breaking when timestamps collide at microsecond resolution. Verified by TestOrderingByDecidedAt::test_out_of_order_decisions_are_sorted_on_output and test_microsecond_tied_timestamps_use_entity_id_tiebreaker; test_pure_helper_does_not_mutate_input pins the immutability contract.

criteria_verification.evidence: The default 'write' parameter is forge.memory.writer.write_entity, awaited directly inside write_session_outcome (no asyncio.ensure_future / no thread). Failures from write_entity propagate to the caller. TestSynchronousWrite::test_write_invoked_with_session_outcome_and_group_id verifies the call shape (PIPELINE_HISTORY_GROUP_ID + built SessionOutcome). test_write_failure_propagates_to_caller verifies the synchronous-failure contract.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Only fires on first terminal-state transition; in-progress states
criteria_verification.evidence: _is_terminal(outcome) is checked before the existence check; non-terminal outcomes (in_progress, running, pending, queued, '', SUCCESS) short-circuit to a logged warning + None return. TestTerminalOnly::test_non_terminal_outcome_returns_none parametrises over six non-terminal strings; test_non_terminal_does_not_query_existence proves the short-circuit happens before any I/O; test_non_terminal_logs_warning_with_build_id verifies the structured log line.
criteria_verification.notes: _is_terminal(outcome) is checked before the existence check; non-terminal outcomes (in_progress, running, pending, queued, '', SUCCESS) short-circuit to a logged warning + None return. TestTerminalOnly::test_non_terminal_outcome_returns_none parametrises over six non-terminal strings; test_non_terminal_does_not_query_existence proves the short-circuit happens before any I/O; test_non_terminal_logs_warning_with_build_id verifies the structured log line.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Concurrent calls (two Forge instances) → both see the same existence
criteria_verification.evidence: _session_outcome_entity_id derives a deterministic UUID5 from build_id under a namespaced URI prefix, so two writers for the same build produce the same entity_id (Graphiti upsert-key dedupe — TASK-IC-001 contract). TestConcurrentDedupeContract: test_entity_id_is_deterministic_for_build_id, test_entity_id_differs_for_distinct_build_ids, test_concurrent_callers_one_writes_one_no_ops (stateful exists-check simulating two-instance race), and test_split_brain_writes_share_entity_id (worst-case both-see-False race verifies the entity_id remains identical).
criteria_verification.notes: _session_outcome_entity_id derives a deterministic UUID5 from build_id under a namespaced URI prefix, so two writers for the same build produce the same entity_id (Graphiti upsert-key dedupe — TASK-IC-001 contract). TestConcurrentDedupeContract: test_entity_id_is_deterministic_for_build_id, test_entity_id_differs_for_distinct_build_ids, test_concurrent_callers_one_writes_one_no_ops (stateful exists-check simulating two-instance race), and test_split_brain_writes_share_entity_id (worst-case both-see-False race verifies the entity_id remains identical).

issues.description:
Advisory (non-blocking): task-work produced a report with 2 of 3 expected agent invocations. Missing phases: 3 (Implementation). Consider invoking these agents via the Task tool to strengthen stack-specific quality:
- Phase 3: `python-api-specialist` (Implementation)
No seam tests detected for cross-boundary feature. Consider adding seam/contract/boundary tests to validate technology boundary interactions. This is a recommendation, not a blocking gate.

rationale: All quality gates passed. Conditionally approved — test collection errors in independent verification. All Player quality gates passed. All acceptance criteria met. Architecture context: 352 chars provided.
